summary: REQ-006 and CDNS-002/AC-006 are not satisfied. Concurrent Get and Put operations access a Go map without synchronization, causing data races and possible fatal runtime failures. Mutable byte slices also cross the cache boundary without copying, allowing caller-driven races and corruption. No concurrency acceptance test is included. Expired entries are never removed, creating a potential memory-exhaustion and performance risk.

findings.claim: Get and Put concurrently access the entries map without synchronization.
findings.expected: All cache operations must be race-free under concurrent use and must not terminate the process.
findings.actual: Get reads c.entries while Put writes it directly. Concurrent execution creates data races and can trigger the unrecoverable runtime failure "concurrent map read and map write".

reproducer.ref: Run goroutines repeatedly calling Get and Put on one Cache under go test -race; the race detector reports conflicting map accesses and execution may fail with concurrent map read and map write.

findings.suggested_remediation: Protect entries with sync.RWMutex, holding a read lock for lookup and a write lock for mutation, or use another correctly synchronized design. Verify concurrent Get/Put workloads with the race detector.

findings.claim: The cache retains and returns caller-owned mutable byte slices, so map locking alone would not make the cache race-safe.
findings.expected: Cached message ownership must prevent callers from concurrently mutating internal cached data or observing unintended mutations.
findings.actual: Put stores msg without copying and Get returns the same backing array. Mutation of the input after Put, or mutation of a Get result by either caller, can race with other users and corrupt cached responses.

reproducer.ref: Put a byte slice, then concurrently mutate the original while another goroutine reads a Get result; go test -race detects accesses to the shared backing array.

findings.suggested_remediation: Clone msg when inserting and return a clone from Get, with the lookup and copy performed under appropriate synchronization. Alternatively, enforce immutable ownership through an API that callers cannot violate.

findings.claim: The candidate contains no concurrency test covering CDNS-002/AC-006.
findings.expected: An acceptance test should exercise concurrent cache operations and pass under go test -race.
findings.actual: The candidate diff adds only cache.go and provides no test demonstrating concurrent safety or detecting the map and slice races.
findings.suggested_remediation: Add a deterministic stress test with concurrent Get and Put operations, varied keys, expiration checks, and shared-slice ownership checks; run it with go test -race.

findings.claim: Expired entries are never removed and the cache has no size bound, allowing memory consumption to grow with unique keys.
findings.expected: A long-lived cache should reclaim expired entries and apply an appropriate capacity or eviction policy when keys can be influenced by requests.
findings.actual: Get reports expired entries as misses but leaves them in entries, while Put continuously adds arbitrary keys. The map can therefore retain expired messages indefinitely.

reproducer.ref: Insert many unique keys with already-expired TTLs, perform Get calls after expiration, and observe that len(c.entries) does not decrease.

findings.suggested_remediation: Remove expired entries safely, add bounded-capacity eviction or periodic cleanup, and define limits suitable for the resolver's exposure to attacker-controlled query names.

unreviewed_areas: Resolver call sites and their guarantees about message immutability were not provided.
Repository-wide cache tests, race-detector configuration, and production eviction controls outside this file were not provided.
evidence_gaps: No go test -race output or concurrency acceptance-test result accompanied the candidate.
The excerpt does not define capacity, eviction, or exact expiration-boundary requirements.
The degree to which cache keys are attacker-controlled cannot be confirmed from the isolated diff.
